action: Inbox operator audit command + extended inject-task collision check (git log + processed/rejected dirs)
operator_note: BLOCKED-BY: c37 ships first (provides inbox-mover.sh + 3-state lifecycle that audit reads). Scope (3 files, ~125 LoC): (1) scripts/utility/inbox-audit.sh NEW ~70 LoC — list every file in inbox/processing/processed/rejected/retry with git-log proof; outputs operator-friendly table with columns: state, task_id, cycle, git_sha, commit_subject; supports --json flag for tooling. Uses git log --grep convention from c37. (2) scripts/utility/inbox-reconcile.sh NEW ~30 LoC — manual recovery escape hatches: --recover-all-orphans (forces inbox-mover.sh recover-orphans even when dispatcher up); --force-skip-shipped <id> (operator-asserted skip when git-log convention was broken, e.g. amended commits with rewritten message); --help. (3) scripts/utility/inject-task.sh MODIFY +25 LoC — extend exit-11 collision check (currently checks state.json:carryoverTodos[] and .evolve/inbox/*.json maxdepth 1) to ALSO check git log via 'git log --grep="^feat: cycle [0-9]+ — ${id}(:| )" --format=%H main | head -1 | grep -q .' AND .evolve/inbox/processed/**/*.json + .evolve/inbox/rejected/**/*.json. Add --force flag to bypass (rare; intentional re-runs with same ID get a new attempt; c37's Layer 1 still immediately moves them to processed/). The user value: operator gets a single command to debug 'what is in my inbox and why' with git-proof justification for every entry.
